# SPEC: #67 — feat: create /ship high-order play for autonomous branch-to-main delivery
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Create the first high-order play in Meridian — `/ship` — that autonomously delivers a feature branch to main by sequencing existing atomic plays (`commit-code`, `create-pr`) with inline agent delegations for review, merge, and return-to-main, with a guardian that defaults to auto-approve and halts only on genuine blockers.

**Constraints:**
- C1: Current branch must NOT be main, master, or develop (hard halt)
- C2: A git remote must be configured (hard halt)
- C3: An issue number must be extractable from the branch name — NWWI (hard halt)
- C4: Orchestrator MUST delegate domain tasks to agents; no direct git/gh execution
- C5: Maximum 5 distinct agents (L2 limit); this play uses exactly 2 (repo-orchestrator, project-orchestrator via commit-code)
- C6: Guardian logic is INLINE in SKILL.md — no dependency on a `workflow-guardian` agent (which does not exist)
- C7: Self-resolution CANNOT change code or documentation; CAN retry operations, create issues, adjust PR metadata, rebase
- C8: Evidence written to `.meridian/{issue}/evidence/ship/{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.md` (per config.yaml STM structure)
- C9: Auto-approve is default; halt only on genuine blockers (merge conflicts, CI failures, protected branch violations, blocker-severity review findings)
- C10: Guardian evaluates inline after each step; does NOT prompt user unless a blocker is detected
- C11: Skip steps whose preconditions are already satisfied — no uncommitted changes skips step 1; PR already exists skips step 2

**Failure Conditions:**
- F1: Current branch is a protected branch — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F2: No git remote configured — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F3: No issue number extractable from branch name — hard halt (pre-flight)
- F4: Merge conflicts that cannot be auto-resolved — halt, create tracking issue
- F5: PR review finds blocker-severity quality items (must_have.status == FAIL) — halt, present to user
- F6: Merge fails after max retry attempts — halt, create tracking issue
- F7: User explicitly cancels at a guardian-halted checkpoint (Vanish)

---

## Summary

The `/ship` play is the first high-order play in Meridian. It chains two existing atomic plays (`commit-code`, `create-pr`) with three inline agent delegations to `repo-orchestrator` (review-PR via `analyze-pr` skill, merge via Bash write exception, return-to-main via Bash write exception). The guardian is implemented as inline decision logic within SKILL.md — not as a separate agent. This explicitly deviates from ADR 003's reference to `workflow-guardian` agent, but the `workflow-guardian` agent does not exist in the codebase, and the ADR's guardian decision criteria are fully implementable inline at the orchestrator level.

Two structural gaps exist that require modifications beyond the new play files. First, `repo-orchestrator` currently permits Bash only for read-only operations — merge (`gh pr merge`) and checkout-then-pull (`git checkout {base} && git pull`) are write operations with no corresponding skills. These must be added as named write exceptions with rationale. Second, both `commit-code` and `create-pr` always checkpoint before their respective operations (commit-code conditionally, create-pr mandatorily per C5). When orchestrated under `/ship`, these checkpoints create friction that breaks the zero-friction intent. The atomic plays must be modified to check for `ship_context.auto_approve: true` and bypass user-facing checkpoints when set, while still writing the audit artifact.

---

## Affected Files

| File | Role | Change Needed |
|------|------|---------------|
| `core/components/plays/ship/SKILL.md` | high-order play execution blueprint | Create new |
| `core/components/plays/ship/reference/intent.yaml` | Operational contract — all constraints and failure conditions | Create new |
| `core/components/plays/ship/templates/checkpoint.md` | STM artifact template following ADR 008 mandatory schema | Create new |
| `core/components/plays/ship/templates/guardian-decision.md` | Guardian auto-approve/halt decision record template | Create new |
| `core/components/plays/ship/templates/final-report.md` | Consolidated delivery evidence report template | Create new |
| `core/components/agents/repo-orchestrator.md` | Agent Bash scope | Modify: add `gh pr merge` and `git checkout {base} && git pull` as named write exceptions |
| `core/components/plays/commit-code/SKILL.md` | L1 commit play — Step 3 checkpoint logic | Modify: add `ship_context.auto_approve` bypass before existing conditional auto-approve |
| `core/components/plays/create-pr/SKILL.md` | L1 PR play — Step 2 checkpoint logic | Modify: add `ship_context.auto_approve` bypass with quality gate before C5 enforcement |
| `docs/components/plays.md` | Play catalog | Modify: add `/ship` as first high-order play entry |

---

## Technical Approach

**Strategy:** Create the `/ship` high-order play following the established `SKILL.md + reference/intent.yaml + templates/` pattern from `create-pr` (explicitly designated as the golden standard in `docs/components/plays.md`). The SKILL.md orchestrator holds the full 7-step workflow (Step 0 pre-flight through Step 6 report) with inline guardian evaluation logic after each consequential step. Steps 1 and 2 invoke the `commit-code` and `create-pr` L1s via the Skill tool, passing `ship_context.auto_approve: true` in play context. Steps 3–5 delegate directly to `repo-orchestrator` with scoped intent bundles. The two critical gaps — merge capability and L1 checkpoint bypass — are resolved as part of implementation.

**Alternatives Considered:**

| Approach | Reason Rejected |
|----------|----------------|
| Invoke `workflow-guardian` agent per ADR 003 | The agent does not exist in `core/components/agents/`. Creating it is out of scope and would consume one of the 5 allowed agent slots, adding complexity with no benefit for a 2-agent play |
| Create new inline merge/checkout steps without agent delegation | Plays forbid direct Bash/git/gh execution — the orchestrator cannot run `gh pr merge`. Must go through repo-orchestrator |
| Create a new `merge-pr` skill in skills/ | The skill would be a thin wrapper around `gh pr merge`. The Bash write exception in repo-orchestrator is simpler and maintains the existing one-skill-per-logical-operation pattern without adding a new skill file |
| Rely on L1's own auto-approve logic without modification | `create-pr` C5 mandates "always checkpoint before PR creation." The conditional auto-approve in `commit-code` does help but does not address all cases. Without explicit `ship_context` bypass, zero-friction intent cannot be achieved |
| Pass `auto_approve: true` via play context without modifying L1s | atomic plays read `reference/intent.yaml` for their behavioral constraints. `ship_context` in the prompt would be visible to the agent but the orchestrator's checkpoint logic needs explicit code-level handling |

**Risks:**

| Risk | Mitigation | Severity |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `repo-orchestrator` has no merge skill; Bash is currently read-only | Add named write exceptions: `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch {pr_number}` and `git checkout {base_branch} && git pull` with inline rationale | High |
| `create-pr` C5 mandates checkpoint always — cannot suppress without L1 modification | Modify create-pr Step 2 to check `ship_context.auto_approve` before C5 enforcement; bypass only when quality is clean (no must_have FAIL) | High |
| ADR 003 references a `workflow-guardian` agent; inline guardian deviates from this | Document the deviation in SKILL.md with explicit rationale. Recommend ADR update as follow-on task. The deviation is already codified in issue C6 | Medium |
| Guardian auto-approve may pass a PR with REVIEW-status items | Guardian evaluates `must_have_fail == 0`; REVIEW items are not blockers — this is intended behavior, documented in edge cases | Low |
| Squash merge loses individual commit history on the branch | Intentional for clean main history; documented as architectural trade-off. Branch is deleted post-merge | Low |
| `git checkout {base} && git pull` may fail on dirty tree in repo-orchestrator | repo-orchestrator self-recovery already handles dirty working tree (stash/pop pattern documented in the agent); no additional logic needed | Low |
